title: Tap. Drop. Sort. — The Most Satisfying 15 Seconds
hook_3s: A finger taps a lavender tray — rainbow marbles cascade onto a conveyor with a soft *plink*, each one snapping into its matching pastel box.
scene_flow: 0–3s HOOK: Close-up on a single tray packed with glossy marbles. A finger taps — marbles tumble in slow-mo onto the conveyor with ASMR plinks. Overlay: 'Can you sort them all?'
3–7s BUILD: Camera pulls back to reveal the full machine. Marbles roll along the belt and *pop* into color-matched boxes — green→green, orange→orange. Counter ticks up. Overlay: 'Match the colors'

7–11s TENSION: A wrong-colored marble approaches the wrong box. Belt slows. Red flash hint: 'Tap to release!' Player taps a second tray just in time — correct marble drops in. Satisfying chime + confetti puff.
11–14s PAYOFF: All boxes fill simultaneously. Boxes glow, marbles sparkle, screen washes in soft lavender-to-orange gradient. Level complete stamp lands with a bouncy squash.
14–15s CTA: Game logo + bright orange 'Play Free' button pulses. Finger taps screen.
visual_direction: Lean fully into Marble Sort's soft pastel identity: lavender (#E5DFFF) background sky, light-grey (#F0F0F8) machine and conveyor with rounded edges and gentle ambient occlusion, and a vibrant orange (#FFB13B) reserved for UI accents, the CTA, and the celebratory burst. Clean 3D cartoon style with chunky rounded shapes, glossy marble specular highlights, and shallow depth-of-field on the hero tap. Motion is slow, weighty, and physics-driven — every marble lands with a squish-and-settle. Sound design is ASMR-forward: plinks, soft thuds, gentle chimes — no aggressive SFX. Camera stays at a comfortable 3/4 top-down so both trays and boxes are always legible.
rationale: We keep the archetype's winning 'tap → snap-into-place → satisfying payoff' rhythm but swap the mismatched crossword shell for Marble Sort's actual core loop, protecting D1 retention while still capturing the satisfaction-seeking 25–55 female audience. Test against a control with no tension beat (scene 3) to isolate whether the near-miss moment lifts IPM, and A/B the hook overlay 'Can you sort them all?' vs 'So satisfying 😌' to see which intent signal converts better.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, vertical 9:16 mobile ad still: a close-up 3/4 view of a glossy light-grey rounded sorting machine on a soft lavender #E5DFFF background, light-grey #F0F0F8 conveyor belt with pastel marbles (mint, pink, sky-blue, orange) tumbling from a tray, a large cartoon finger tapping the tray, color-matched rounded boxes glowing below, vibrant orange #FFB13B headline text 'Can you sort them all?' at top, clean and colorful 3D cartoon style with soft rounded shapes, soft studio lighting, shallow depth of field, ASMR mood

Storyboard frame 2, vertical 9:16: top-down 3D cartoon view of a pastel sorting machine mid-action, glossy marbles rolling along a light-grey #F0F0F8 conveyor belt against a lavender #E5DFFF backdrop, marbles snapping into color-matched rounded boxes with tiny sparkle bursts, bold orange #FFB13B overlay text 'Match the colors' centered, clean and colorful 3D cartoon style with soft rounded shapes, calm satisfying lighting

Storyboard payoff frame, vertical 9:16: celebratory hero shot of all pastel boxes filled with sorted glossy marbles glowing softly, confetti puffs in lavender #E5DFFF and orange #FFB13B, light-grey #F0F0F8 machine framing the scene, big bouncy orange #FFB13B stamp text 'Level 1 Complete!' across the middle, clean and colorful 3D cartoon style with soft rounded shapes, joyful satisfying mood
